en: For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in relation to God, to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
zh: 每一個大祭司都是從人間選出來，奉派替人辦理與 神有關的事，為的是要獻上禮物和贖罪的祭物。

en: He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is beset with weakness.
zh: 他能夠溫和地對待那些無知和迷誤的人，因為他自己也被軟弱所困。

en: Because of this he is obligated to offer sacrifice for his own sins just as he does for those of the people.
zh: 因此，他怎樣為人民的罪獻祭，也應該怎樣為自己的罪獻祭。

en: And no one takes this honor for himself, but only when called by God, just as Aaron was.
zh: 沒有人可以自己取得這大祭司的尊榮，只有像亞倫一樣，蒙 神選召的才可以。

en: So also Christ did not exalt himself to be made a high priest, but was appointed by him who said to him, "You are my Son, today I have begotten you";
zh: 照樣，基督也沒有自己爭取作大祭司的尊榮，而是曾經對他說： “你是我的兒子， 我今日生了你” 的 神榮耀了他；

en: as he says also in another place, "You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek."
zh: 就像他在另一處說： “你是照著麥基洗德的體系， 永遠作祭司的。”

en: In the days of his flesh, Jesus offered up prayers and supplications, with loud cries and tears, to him who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his reverence.
zh: 基督在世的時候，曾經流淚大聲禱告懇求那位能救他脫離死亡的 神；因著他的敬虔，就蒙了應允。

en: Although he was a son, he learned obedience through what he suffered.
zh: 他雖然是兒子，還是因著所受的苦難學會了順從。

en: And being made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him,
zh: 他既然順從到底 ，就成了所有順從他的人得到永遠救恩的根源；

en: being designated by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
zh: 而且蒙 神照著麥基洗德的體系，稱他為大祭司。

en: About this we have much to say, and it is hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
zh: 論到這些事，我們有很多話要說，可是很難解釋；因為你們已經遲鈍了，聽不進去。

en: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic principles of the oracles of God. You need milk, not solid food,
zh: 到這個時候，你們應該已經作老師了；可是你們還需要有人再把 神道理的初步教導你們。你們成了只能吃奶而不能吃乾糧的人！

en: for everyone who lives on milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness, since he is a child.
zh: 凡是吃奶的，還是個嬰孩，對公義的道理沒有經歷；

en: But solid food is for the mature, for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil.
zh: 只有長大成人的，才能吃乾糧，他們的官能因為操練純熟，就能分辨是非了。
